novel_features.name: SERP rank-drift diff
novel_features.description: Diff a Google query's two most recent stored SERPs and see exactly which results moved, appeared, or dropped — entirely offline, no credits spent.
novel_features.rationale: Only possible with a local snapshot history keyed by query and params; the live API has no memory, so rank deltas require stored SERPs no other Scrape.do tool keeps.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to know whether a ranking changed since last check, this answers it without re-spending a 10-credit SERP call.

novel_features.name: Multi-agent safe fan-out
novel_features.description: Dispatch a list of URLs or queries through a shared concurrency lease and per-call credit ledger, auto-retrying only the non-billed 429/502/510 classes and stopping before a credit ceiling.
novel_features.rationale: A SQLite-backed shared lease caps any number of independent agent processes at the account's live concurrent-request limit — impossible without shared local state the API itself doesn't provide.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent swarm hammer one account at full speed without 429 storms or blowing the monthly budget.

novel_features.name: Local credit ledger + spend forecast
novel_features.description: Attribute spend by mode and query-family from a local ledger debited off the authoritative per-call cost header, joined with cached account state to forecast burn-rate against days remaining.
novel_features.rationale: The authoritative cost lives in the Scrape.do-Request-Cost response header and is discarded after each call; persisting it locally is the only way to attribute and forecast spend the live monthly counter can't.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent how much budget is left and which workloads are eating it before the account hits a hard 401.

novel_features.name: Pre-flight cost estimator
novel_features.description: Print the exact credit cost a request will incur — accounting for render, super-proxy, Google endpoints, and per-domain overrides — before spending a single credit.
novel_features.rationale: A local credit-table lookup (datacenter 1, +render 5, super 10, super+render 25, Google 10, domain overrides) that never touches the API, so an agent can decide before it spends.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent compare the cost of cheap vs expensive scrape modes and pick the cheapest path that works.

novel_features.name: Cross-query movers digest
novel_features.description: Scan every tracked query's latest-versus-previous SERP snapshot and surface only the queries whose top positions moved past a threshold.
novel_features.rationale: A local aggregation across all stored snapshots that no single SERP API call can produce — it compares history, not a single live response.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Turns hundreds of tracked keywords into a short 'what changed this week' list an agent can act on.

narrative.headline: The first CLI for Scrape-do with Google SERP scraping plus a credit and concurrency governor
narrative.value_prop: Every Scrape.do surface — the core scraper plus the whole Google family (search, maps, news, shopping, flights, hotels, trends) — wrapped with an offline SQLite history. The governor estimates credit cost before every call with `cost`, debits a local ledger from the authoritative cost header with `budget`, and gates concurrent requests against your plan's live ceiling so an agent swarm never 429s itself or burns the monthly budget. SERPs become queryable history: `drift` and `movers` surface rank changes offline with no re-spend.
narrative.auth_narrative: Scrape.do uses a single API token passed as the `token` query parameter. Set it as `SCRAPEDO_API_KEY` in your environment and the CLI never logs the value. The same token drives the core scraper and every Google and Ready-API endpoint.

quickstart.comment: Confirm SCRAPEDO_API_KEY is set and the API is reachable.
The primary workflow: a structured Google SERP, stored locally.
See remaining credits, concurrency headroom, and burn-rate forecast.
Run the search again later, then diff the rank changes offline.
General page scrape with JS rendering, returned as clean markdown.

troubleshoots.symptom: 429 Too Many Requests during a fan-out
troubleshoots.fix: You hit the plan's concurrent-request cap; the lease backs off automatically — lower throughput with `batch --max-concurrency <N>` or upgrade the plan.
Scrape.do returns 401 when monthly credits are exhausted or the subscription is suspended; check `scrape-do-pp-cli budget` and the dashboard.
export SCRAPEDO_API_KEY=<your-token> (the value is never logged); re-run doctor.

troubleshoots.symptom: A render request returned raw HTML instead of JSON
troubleshoots.fix: returnJSON requires render — pass both --return-json and --render.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for this CLI whenever a task needs Google search results, structured SERP data, or any proxied web scrape via Scrape.do — and especially when several agents share one Scrape.do account. Its governor (cost/budget/batch) keeps concurrent usage inside the plan's limits and the monthly credit budget, and its offline SERP history (drift/movers) answers rank-change questions without re-spending credits. Prefer it over raw curl calls when you care about credit cost, concurrency safety, or comparing results over time.

recipes.title: Narrow a deep SERP payload for an agent
recipes.explanation: The SERP JSON is large and deeply nested; --select with dotted paths returns just rank, title, and link so an agent doesn't burn context parsing the full payload.

recipes.title: Estimate cost before an expensive scrape
recipes.explanation: Prints the expected credits (LinkedIn domain override + render + super proxy) with no API spend, so you can choose the cheapest mode that works.

recipes.title: Fan out a URL list under the concurrency cap
recipes.explanation: Dispatches every URL through the shared concurrency lease, retries only the non-billed 429/502/510 classes, and stops before the 500-credit ceiling.

recipes.title: See what ranked-changes happened this week
recipes.explanation: Compares each tracked query's two latest stored SERPs and lists only the queries whose top positions moved by 3 or more — entirely offline.

recipes.title: Query your stored SERP history with SQL
recipes.command: scrape-do-pp-cli sql "SELECT domain, COUNT(*) c FROM serp_organic GROUP BY domain ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10"
recipes.explanation: Read-only SQL over the local store — share-of-voice by domain across every stored SERP, with no credit spent.
